word_list.word: clergymen's sons always turn out badly
word_list.pronunciation: klˈɜːdʒɪmˌɛnz sˈʌnz ˈɔːlweɪz tˈɜːn ˈaʊt bˈædli

senses.definition: used to imply that the burden of high expectations placed on the children of successful or accomplished individuals can be damaging and lead to rebellion or negative behavior

word_list.word: doctors make the worst patients
word_list.pronunciation: dˈɑːktɚz mˌeɪk ðə wˈɜːst pˈeɪʃənts

senses.definition: used to imply that those who work in a certain profession may be difficult clients or recipients of services related to that profession due to their preconceived notions and knowledge of the field

word_list.word: every light has its shadow
word_list.pronunciation: ˈɛvɹi lˈaɪt hɐz ɪts ʃˈædoʊ

senses.definition: used to imply that in any situation or experience, there are both positive and negative aspects that coexist

word_list.word: every white has its black, and every sweet its sour
word_list.pronunciation: ˈɛvɹi wˈaɪt hɐz ɪts blˈæk ænd ˈɛvɹi swˈiːt ɪts sˈaɪʊɹ

senses.definition: used to emphasize that every positive or desirable thing is accompanied by its contrasting or negative counterpart

word_list.word: extremes meet
word_list.pronunciation: ɛkstɹˈiːmz mˈiːt

senses.definition: used to suggest that two seemingly opposite or extreme things or people can actually have some common ground or similarity, despite their apparent differences

word_list.word: from the sublime to the ridiculous is only (a|one) step
word_list.pronunciation: fɹʌmðə sʌblˈaɪm tə ðə ɹɪdˈɪkjʊləs ɪz ˈoʊnli ɐ wˈʌn stˈɛp

senses.definition: used to imply that it is possible for something that is very serious or profound to quickly and easily become something trivial or absurd, often with little warning or explanation

examples.x: The comedian joked that from the sublime to the ridiculous is only a step, and that the best comedy often came from taking serious topics and turning them on their head.

word_list.word: honey is sweet, but the bee stings
word_list.pronunciation: hˈʌni ɪz swˈiːt bˌʌt ðə bˈiː stˈɪŋz

senses.definition: used for saying that something may appear pleasant or desirable on the surface, but it can have negative or harmful consequences as well

word_list.word: one cannot love and be wise
word_list.phonetic_text: n kæn.nɑt lʌv ænd bi: waɪz
word_list.phonetic_am_text: n kān.naat lav ānd bi vaiz
word_list.pronunciation: wˈʌn kænˈɑːt lˈʌv ænd biː wˈaɪz

senses.definition: used to imply the idea that love is often associated with strong emotions and impulsive behavior, which can cloud a person's judgment and lead them to behave foolishly or make unwise decisions

word_list.word: opposites (often|) (tend to|) attract
word_list.pronunciation: ˈɑːpəsˌɪts ˈɔfən tˈɛnd tʊ ɐtɹˈækt

senses.definition: used to suggest that people are often drawn to those who are different from themselves in terms of personality, interests, or other characteristics

word_list.word: the road to hell is paved with good intentions
word_list.pronunciation: ðə ɹˈoʊd tə hˈɛl ɪz pˈeɪvd wɪð ɡˈʊd ɪntˈɛnʃənz

senses.definition: used to imply that even if one's intentions are good, one's actions may still lead to negative consequences, and that one must be mindful of the impact of one's actions on others

examples.x: The historian noted that the road to hell is paved with good intentions, and that many historical atrocities and injustices were carried out by individuals who believed they were acting for the greater good.

word_list.word: the shoemaker's son always goes barefoot
word_list.pronunciation: ðə ʃˈuːmeɪkɚz sˈʌn ˈɔːlweɪz ɡoʊz bˈɛɹfʊt

senses.definition: used to imply that professionals or experts in a particular field may neglect to use their skills or services for their own benefit or that of their family members

word_list.word: the spirit is willing, but the flesh is weak
word_list.pronunciation: ðə spˈɪɹɪt ɪz wˈɪlɪŋ bˌʌt ðə flˈɛʃ ɪz wˈiːk

senses.definition: used to suggest that even though a person may have the desire or intention to do something, their physical weakness or limitations may prevent them from actually carrying it out

word_list.word: there is no joy without alloy
word_list.pronunciation: ðɛɹ ɪz nˈoʊ dʒˈɔɪ wɪðˌaʊt ˈælɔɪ

senses.definition: used to suggest that even the happiest experiences in life come with some negative aspects or challenges

word_list.word: wanton kittens make sober cats
word_list.pronunciation: wˈɑːntən kˈɪʔn̩z mˌeɪk sˈoʊbɚ kˈæts

senses.definition: used to describe the behavior of young people, who may be impulsive and reckless in their youth but become more responsible and sensible as they get older

word_list.word: where God builds a church, the devil will build a chapel
word_list.pronunciation: wˌɛɹ ɡˈɑːd bˈɪldz ɐ tʃˈɜːtʃ ðə dˈɛvəl wɪl bˈɪld ɐ tʃˈæpəl

senses.definition: used to suggest that wherever there is progress or success, there will always be those who seek to exploit or corrupt it

word_list.word: God sends meat (and|as) the devil sends cooks
word_list.pronunciation: ɡˈɑːd sˈɛndz mˈiːt ænd æz ðə dˈɛvəl sˈɛndz kˈʊks

senses.definition: used to warn against being misled by tempting but ultimately harmful or unnecessary things, emphasizing that God provides what is needed while the devil tempts with what is wanted

word_list.word: fire is a good servant but a bad master
word_list.pronunciation: fˈaɪɚɹ ɪz ɐ ɡˈʊd sˈɜːvənt bˌʌt ɐ bˈæd mˈæstɚ

senses.definition: used to imply that while certain things can be beneficial when used properly, they can become harmful or dangerous if they are not controlled or managed effectively

word_list.word: go abroad and you will hear news of home
word_list.phonetic_text: goʊ əb.rɔd ænd ju wɪl hɪr nu:z ʌv hoʊm
word_list.phonetic_am_text: gooo ēb.rawd ānd yoo vil hir nooz av hooom
word_list.pronunciation: ɡˌoʊ ɐbɹˈɔːd ænd juː wɪl hˈɪɹ nˈuːz ʌv hˈoʊm

senses.definition: used to suggest that being away from home can make a person more interested in news from their home and family, as they are no longer directly involved in the daily events

word_list.word: one man's loss is another man's gain
word_list.pronunciation: wˈʌn mˈænz lˈɔs ɪz ɐnˈʌðɚ mˈænz ɡˈeɪn

senses.definition: used to imply that resources and opportunities are finite, and that one person's loss can create an opportunity for someone else to succeed

word_list.word: the cobbler's wife is the worst shod
word_list.pronunciation: ðə kˈɑːblɚz wˈaɪf ɪz ðə wˈɜːst ʃˈɑːd

senses.definition: used to suggest that professionals or experts in a particular field may neglect to apply their skills or services to their own lives or those of their immediate family

word_list.word: (everyone|everybody) wants to go to heaven, but (nobody|no one) wants to die
word_list.pronunciation: ˈɛvɹɪwˌʌn ˈɛvɹɪbˌɑːdi wˈɑːnts tə ɡˌoʊ tə hˈɛvən bˌʌt nˈoʊbɑːdi nˈoʊwˈʌn wˈɑːnts tə dˈaɪ

senses.definition: used to suggest that people often desire the rewards or benefits of a particular goal but are not willing to take the necessary steps or make the necessary sacrifices to achieve it

word_list.word: the weakness of the enemy makes our strength
word_list.pronunciation: ðə wˈiːknəs ʌvðɪ ˈɛnəmi mˌeɪks ˌaʊɚ stɹˈɛŋθ

senses.definition: used to suggest that the vulnerabilities of one's opponent can provide an opportunity for one's own strength or power to be demonstrated

word_list.word: every rose has its thorn
word_list.pronunciation: ˈɛvɹi ɹˈoʊz hɐz ɪts θˈɔːɹn

senses.definition: used to imply that every good thing in life has its own negative aspects or challenges

examples.x: Sarah knew that starting her own business would be challenging, but she also understood that every rose has its thorn and was prepared to face any difficulties that came her way.
